prevalence.value: ~0.7-1.0% chronic HBV (HBsAg prevalence; varies by country)
prevalence.source: ECDC hepatitis B surveillance / WHO Europe
prevalence.trend: stable to decreasing with vaccination and harm reduction; higher prevalence in some migrant groups

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: HBsAg and HBV DNA for diagnosis/staging; ALT and fibrosis assessment; management per EASL guidelines

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: antenatal screening common; targeted screening in migrants and risk groups varies by country
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; diagnosis and linkage-to-care gaps persist especially in marginalized populations

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: EASL-aligned workup with HBV DNA and fibrosis staging widely available in specialist care
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: First-line tenofovir or entecavir; universal infant vaccination in most member states; PMTCT protocols and HBIG where indicated
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Undiagnosed infections in migrants and marginalized groups and variability in screening policies across member states
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong vaccination programmes, high access to antivirals, and standardized specialist care guidelines (EASL)
